---
name: jupyterbook-formatting
description: Make a user-friendly book from Python scripts/notebooks
compatibility: opencode
---


This is a tool to make reports out of ipynbs and markdown. The project config will look something like this:

```
# See docs at: https://mystmd.org/guide/frontmatter
version: 1
project:
  id: a7fc69f8-fbaf-4b89-b98e-b4725125aec3
  # title:
  # description:
  # keywords: []
  # authors: []
  github: https://github.com/thadryanjs/dotfiles
  # To autogenerate a Table of Contents, run "jupyter book init --write-toc"
  toc:
    # Auto-generated by `myst init --write-toc`
    - file: SKILL.md

site:
  template: book-theme
  # options:
  #   favicon: favicon.ico
  #   logo: site_logo.png
```

The table of context (TOC) determines which ipynbs go into the final book.

To keep the project orderly, change the script names to look like this:


```
00_initial-something.ipynb
01_second-something.ipynb
02_third-something.ipynb
```

...which would give us a table of contents of:


```
...
  toc:
    # Auto-generated by `myst init --write-toc`
    - file: 00_initial-something.ipynb
    - file: 01_second-something.ipynb
    - file: 02_third-something.ipynb
```

Really long projects might need to use a format like this:

```
000_part-a-initial-something.ipynb
001_part-a-second-something.ipynb
002_part-a-third-something.ipynb

100_part-b-initial-something.ipynb
101_part-b-second-something.ipynb
102_part-b-third-something.ipynb

200_part-c-initial-something.ipynb
201_part-c-second-something.ipynb
202_part-c-third-something.ipynb
```
